nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria is protein-rich from slow-braised beef and provides iron and B vitamins. The braising process can concentrate fat and sodium in the consommé; using leaner cuts, trimming visible fat, and skimming fat from the braising liquid reduce saturated fat. Serving on corn tortillas and adding a cabbage slaw or beans increases fiber and micronutrients. Limit added cheese or serve it sparingly to keep saturated fat lower.

to.name: queso fresco or shredded melting cheese (optional)

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Prepare a rich birria by braising beef (chuck or short rib) with dried chiles (guajillo, ancho), garlic, onion, tomato, and warm spices until very tender. Shred the meat and keep the braising liquid (consommé). Warm corn tortillas, dip each briefly into the consommé, fry lightly on a hot skillet until pliable, then fill with shredded birria meat and melt a little cheese if desired. Serve with small bowls of consommé for dipping, chopped onion, fresh cilantro, lime wedges, and optional pickled red onions or salsa. Pair with a simple cabbage slaw or black beans for balance.
